domain: Foraminifera (planktonic + benthic) carbonate biological pump — single-cell organisms build calcite shells from seawater, then sink to deep ocean upon death, sequestering carbon long-term.
specific_mechanism: CaCO₃ uptake into shell (test); progressive shell growth over lifetime; post-mortem shells sink and accumulate as ocean-floor sediment, sequestering carbon for thousands of years.
llm_application: FORAM-ACC: gradient sequestration training schedule. Each mini-batch gradient is accumulated into a per-layer 'sequestration buffer' (ΔW_buffer). When buffer ||ΔW||_F exceeds a sediment-volume threshold, the layer's accumulated update is committed to weights AND the layer is frozen (no further gradient flow). Layers freeze bottom-up as gradient sediment accumulates.
novelty_claim: Gradient accumulation merges into a single update. Layer freezing schedules are explicit per-layer. Progressive freezing exists (e.g., RecAdam). FORAM-ACC's sediment-volume-triggered freeze is novel in formulation but mechanism is in established progressive-freezing space.
